danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct Robot Master character sprites with unique silhouettes and color schemes (Burst Man, Cloud Man, Junk Man, Freeze Man, Spring Man, Slash Man, Shade Man, Turbo Man)
danger_signals.meaning: Boss encounter imminent; stage-specific threats and environmental hazards tied to robot type

danger_signals.visual_form: Black and red robot with wolf companion; distinctive rival character model
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile rival encounter; deceptive threat (appears as ally, later revealed as enemy)

danger_signals.visual_form: Wrecked cityscape, ruins visible during car ride intro; Dr. Wily's escaped state
danger_signals.meaning: World state: containment failure; main threat has been liberated and is active

danger_signals.visual_form: Four initial Robot Masters shown activating; army being rounded up by them
danger_signals.meaning: Coordinated enemy force deployed; larger threat scale than single robot

danger_signals.visual_form: Unused object graphics for Shade Man petrification state; visual effect around Mega Man
danger_signals.meaning: Status effect danger; combat state alteration threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Lab environment; Dr. Light character model
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; healing/repair location; trusted ally headquarters

safety_signals.visual_form: Shop interface accessible from level select; friendly NPC (Auto/Eddie)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition point; safe transaction zone; upgrade opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Roll character in car ride intro sequence
safety_signals.meaning: Trusted ally; civilian support character; indicates mission briefing/departure

safety_signals.visual_form: Rush character sprite and icon in weapon/item menu
safety_signals.meaning: Helper tool availability; support capability; navigational aid (Rush Search)
Progression reward; access to Rush Super Adapter (empowerment signal)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Buster arm cannon; Rush symbol
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dr. Light's lab setting; safe zones; shop operations

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied per Robot Master (Burst Man, Cloud Man, Junk Man, Freeze Man, Spring Man, Slash Man, Shade Man, Turbo Man each have distinct colors)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dr. Wily's prison escape; coordinated robot army insignia
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each Robot Master has unique silhouette, weapon visual, stage aesthetic matching their theme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ruined cityscape; Wily's hidden laboratory; stage environments themed to robot type

faction_indicators.color_identity: Black with red accents (contrasts with Mega Man's blue)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bass robot model; Treble wolf companion
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bass Treble fusion state (shown in unused animations); distinct from Mega Man despite similar capabilities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Encounters during gameplay; later revealed in Dr. Light's lab (injured state); Wily's ally locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dr. Wily imprisoned; society functioning normally (referenced in plot setup)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Backup Robot Masters dormant; no enemy activity; normal city state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cityscape in ruins; wreckage visible during intro car sequence; Dr. Wily at large
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robot Masters actively rampage; city under siege; population in danger; Mega Man mobilized; Roll and Auto assisting in retrieval mission

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Second set of Robot Masters become accessible; stage select updates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression unlock system; new threat wave dispatched; Dr. Wily escalates response

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bass shown in berserk state in Dr. Light's lab; visual state change from ally appearance to enemy confirmation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: False ally becomes active enemy; double-cross moment; threat realignment

environmental_storytelling.technique: Thematic stage design per Robot Master
environmental_storytelling.example: Burst Man stage, Cloud Man stage, Junk Man stage, Freeze Man stage etc. each have visual aesthetics matching robot theme—junk environments, weather effects, elemental hazards corresponding to robot master identity
Intro car ride through wrecked city shows immediate consequences of prison break; visual damage state communicates threat scope before any combat
Dr. Light's lab as safe zone hub; Auto/Eddie shop accessibility from level select (no danger in safe space); Roll present only in intro (support role defined spatially)
Each of 8 Robot Masters has unique color palette, weapon visual, and sprite design; allows instant threat identification and stage prediction through character appearance alone
Visual of four initial robots activating as backup protocol; explains coordinated wave threat; shows pre-planned contingency strategy through stage progression unlock system

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stage-specific attack patterns and environmental hazards; weak to specific Master Weapon (communicated through dialogue/strategic gameplay discovery)
Engage Mega Man in combat briefly, then retreat; verbally claim to also oppose Wily; create false alliance impression
Bass berserk state; leaves battlefield; later confirmed as Wily's operative; injectable into remaining combat encounters
Arrives by car with Roll and Auto; pursues Dr. Wily; defeats Robot Masters sequentially; demonstrates charging buster and slide mechanics
Deploys Robot Masters in waves; coordinates second four-robot wave after first is defeated; remains in command structure despite imprisonment narrative
Constantly rocks back and forth (idle animation); executes tasks when called (Rush Search, Super Adapter combination); visual availability in menu indicates readiness
Operates cybernetic shop; accepts bolts as currency; provides item transactions; stationary in shop interface (non-combatant role)

notes: Mega Man 7 communicates world state and danger primarily through: (1) Robot Master visual distinctiveness creating instant threat identification, (2) environmental storytelling via stage design matching robot theme, (3) faction colors and character design (Mega Man blue vs. varied Robot Master colors vs. Bass black/red contrast), (4) narrative progression gates (first 4 vs. second 4 Robot Masters) reflecting world threat escalation, (5) safe-zone design (Dr. Light's lab, shop accessibility) contrasted with ruined cityscape for danger state, (6) character positioning and availability (Roll only in intro, Bass shifting from ally to enemy state), (7) unused animations suggesting intended petrification and status effects as danger signals. The game relies heavily on sprite-based visual communication and stage accessibility as world-state indicators, with minimal abstract UI communication of faction alignment.
